mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad economic strength, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns as investors price in a robust growth outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth post-2009
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a key threshold after a prolonged period near zero, it signals a committed tightening cycle, increasing the discount rate for equities and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed hiked rates in Dec 2015 for the first time since 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in the 60 trading days following the FEDFUNDS rate exceeding 0.50% in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in retail sales growth and a rise in the unemployment rate indicates a sharp deterioration in consumer health, signaling an imminent economic downturn and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2pp AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0%
failure_condition: The combined signal does not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with an accuracy statistically greater than a random walk (50%).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a period of low inflation points to weakening aggregate demand rather than supply-side constraints, leading to lower earnings expectations and negative stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been weak in early 2016
failure_condition: In low inflation periods, negative INDPRO shocks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a level perceived as the natural rate (NAIRU), it signals impending wage inflation and a more aggressive Federal Reserve tightening path, leading to equity market declines.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% is near historical full employment levels
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.8% and falling.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer confidence and investment in a key cyclical sector, which has positive spillover effects on the broader economy and supports higher equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month momentum of HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is low, an acceleration in CPI inflation signals robust demand and corporate pricing power, boosting nominal earnings and equity valuations. This effect persists until the Fed begins aggressive tightening.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 2%, instances of accelerating CPI are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Higher-than-expected real GDP growth (GDPC1) directly translates into higher aggregate corporate revenues and earnings, leading to upward revisions in equity valuations and positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to the quarterly YoY % change in GDPC1 is not significantly positive over the full sample period.
